input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

samples.output: <think>A man wearing a yellow beanie and black apron stands beside a woman in a black graphic tee behind a large orange pumpkin on a counter. The man gestures with his right hand, moving it from near his temple to extending forward toward the viewer. The woman stands still with her hands resting on the counter. Kitchen cabinets and a microwave are visible in the background behind them.</think><answer></answer>

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text: In a kitchen with wooden cabinets and a microwave, a man in a yellow beanie and black apron stands beside a woman in a black graphic tee behind a large orange pumpkin. [0-4] The man gestures from his temple outward then to his chest while the woman rests her hands on the counter. [4-8] Both look down at the pumpkin before the man lowers his hand and turns to the woman, who looks up at him. [8-12] The man gestures around the pumpkin with both hands, then grasps its sides while looking at the woman. Visible text includes "CAFE", "McCormick", and a sign reading "Your Opinion...".

[14-18] The man wearing a yellow beanie, glasses, and black apron faces forward, smiling and speaking while holding the large orange pumpkin. The woman in the black shirt with a lion graphic stands to his left, looking forward. [18-22] The man looks down smiling, then raises his head to speak, shifting hands near the stem. The woman turns to gaze at him. [22-26] The man lifts his right hand to gesture near his chest, looking from the pumpkin to the woman, who watches him. The static kitchen background includes wooden cabinets, a microwave, a skull decoration, and signs reading "CAR" and "Opinion".

The man in the yellow beanie places his right hand on the large orange pumpkin alongside his left hand [26-28]. He raises his right hand near his face, gesturing with an open palm and curled fingers while speaking, as the woman in the black shirt looks forward [28-32]. He briefly gestures to his chest with his thumb before gripping the pumpkin's side again [32-36]. Finally, a black t-shirt graphic overlays the scene displaying "HAVE YOU SEEN THIS TROLL?" above a blue user icon and a red YouTube subscribe button [36-38].

recent_thinks: [12-14] The man in the yellow beanie maintains his grip on the large orange pumpkin with both hands while looking at the woman. The woman in the black shirt continues to gaze at him, closing her eyes briefly in the final frame. The kitchen setting remains unchanged with cabinets and microwave visible.

[38-40] A static black t-shirt graphic remains centered on a white background, flanked by wooden kitchen cabinets. The shirt features white text asking "HAVE YOU SEEN THIS TROLL?" above a blue silhouette icon and a red YouTube subscribe button showing zero subscribers. The image remains unchanged throughout the duration.

[40-42] The static black t-shirt graphic remains centered on a white background, flanked by wooden kitchen cabinets on either side. White text reads "HAVE YOU SEEN THIS TROLL?" above a blue user silhouette icon and a red YouTube subscribe button showing zero subscribers. No visual changes occur as the image stays completely still.

queries.question: What will the man in the yellow beanie do next? A. Hold the pumpkin with both hands B. Open the small glass jar C. Pick up the red book D. Pick up the spice jar

samples.output: <think>The static black t-shirt graphic remains centered on a white background, flanked by wooden kitchen cabinets visible on the sides. White text reads "HAVE YOU SEEN THIS TROLL?" above a blue user silhouette icon and a red YouTube subscribe button showing zero subscribers. No visual changes occur as the image stays completely still.</think><answer></answer>

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text: In a kitchen with wooden cabinets and a microwave, a man in a yellow beanie and black apron stands beside a woman in a black graphic tee behind a large orange pumpkin. [0-4] The man gestures from his temple outward then to his chest while the woman rests her hands on the counter. [4-8] Both look down at the pumpkin before the man lowers his hand and turns to the woman, who looks up at him. [8-12] The man gestures around the pumpkin with both hands, then grasps its sides while looking at the woman. Visible text includes "CAFE", "McCormick", and a sign reading "Your Opinion...". [14-18] The man wearing a yellow beanie, glasses, and black apron faces forward, smiling and speaking while holding the large orange pumpkin. The woman in the black shirt with a lion graphic stands to his left, looking forward. [18-22] The man looks down smiling, then raises his head to speak, shifting hands near the stem. The woman turns to gaze at him. [22-26] The man lifts his right hand to gesture near his chest, looking from the pumpkin to the woman, who watches him. The static kitchen background includes wooden cabinets, a microwave, a

[112-114] Hands separate flat, pale seeds from fibrous orange pumpkin pulp, dropping extracted seeds onto a pile on a grey plastic sheet beside a stationary large orange pumpkin. [114-120] The scene transitions to a clear plastic bowl filled with water and cleaned pumpkin seeds resting on an orange leaf-patterned tablecloth. A hand enters from the top right, hovering over the rim with fingers extended downward toward the surface. Two seeds float near the edge while the majority remain submerged at the bottom. The hand maintains its position above the container without touching the water or seeds.

From 120-122s, a clear plastic bowl filled with water and pumpkin seeds sits stationary on an orange leaf-patterned tablecloth, with a hand hovering nearby. At 122s, a yellow plastic colander appears to the left of the bowl while hands gesture above the containers. This setup remains static until 126s. Finally, from 126-128s, the scene shifts to a white towel where a pile of cleaned beige pumpkin seeds rests on the right side, with two isolated seeds on the left and fingers visible at the top edge.

[128-132] A hand reaches into a pile of beige pumpkin seeds on a white towel, lifting a small handful to display them in an open palm, while two solitary seeds remain on the left. [132-136] Hands grasp the upper corners of the white towel spread on an orange leaf-patterned surface, folding the fabric inward from the sides to begin enclosing the central pile of seeds. [136-138] The towel is spread flat again with seeds distributed across the center; a dark purple bowl holding a yellow cube appears in the upper right, and a hand on the left holds a small white object near the towel edge.

[138-140] A dark purple bowl with a yellow cube is held above a white towel where beige pumpkin seeds are spread; a hand holds a small object near the edge. [140-142] A maroon bowl and silver spoon sit on the left as hands gather the towel around the seeds. [142-144] Hands lift the bunched towel enclosing the seeds. [144-146] The towel is tilted, pouring the seeds into the maroon bowl. [146-148] The empty towel is laid flat on the orange leaf-patterned tablecloth next to the bowl now containing the seeds.

recent_thinks: [12-14] The man in the yellow beanie maintains his grip on the large orange pumpkin with both hands while looking at the woman. The woman in the black shirt continues to gaze at him, closing her eyes briefly in the final frame. The kitchen setting remains unchanged with cabinets and microwave visible.

[46-48] The black t-shirt graphic overlay vanishes, revealing the kitchen scene. The man in the yellow beanie and black apron stands beside the woman in the black shirt behind the large orange pumpkin. He gestures with his right hand over the pumpkin while looking down.

[80-82] A person in a black shirt saws a knife into a large orange pumpkin resting on grey plastic. Another individual wearing a grey shirt and black apron enters from the right, placing both hands on the pumpkin's side to stabilize it during the cutting process.

[148-150] A maroon bowl filled with beige pumpkin seeds sits on an orange leaf-patterned tablecloth next to a silver spoon. To the right, a white towel rests on the table. A person's hands are visible; the left hand is positioned near the bowl's edge, and the right hand rests on the white towel.

[150-152] The maroon bowl of seeds remains stationary next to a silver spoon on the orange leaf-patterned surface. The person's right hand, previously resting flat, now grasps and bunches the white towel on the right side, lifting the fabric slightly while the left hand hovers near the bowl.

samples.output: <think>A maroon bowl filled with beige seeds sits on an orange leaf-patterned tablecloth next to a silver spoon. To the right, a white towel lies bunched on the surface. The person's hands release both the bowl and towel, resting flat on the tablecloth on either side of the objects.</think><answer></answer>

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).
